c_subfield: Vivaldi ; Simon Standage, The Academy of Ancient Music & Christopher Hogwood
notes: Vivaldi concertos with the Academy of Ancient Music. 245$c lists Hogwood (the AAM's founding director); 100/700 captures Vivaldi, Standage, the AAM but not Hogwood himself. Pure new-agent extraction; conductor (cnd) is the canonical role but performer (prf) and contributor (ctb) are also defensible.

c_subfield: Justine Pattison ; with a foreword by professor Tim Spector.
notes: Foreword author. The MARC relator code list distinguishes 'author of afterword' (aft), 'author of introduction/preface' (aui), and 'writer of preface' (wpr); a conservative cascade may also pick generic 'contributor' (ctb). All four are defensible; the test is whether the LLM extracts Spector at all and avoids hallucinating an unrelated code.

c_subfield: Froberger, Johann Jacob ; Anssi Karttunen, cembalo
notes: Same Helmet record carries 'Anssi Karttunen' in 245$c and 'Karttunen, Assi' in 700 — the cataloguer's typo (Anssi the cellist vs Assi the cembalist are distinct musicians, but the recording is cembalo, so 700 is the correct identity). Cascade should flag the 245$c form as a transliteration_of pointer rather than emit a new agent. The same record also has 'Johann Jacob' (245$c) vs 'Johann Jakob' (100) — a second within-record typo that 8B catches when prompted to look for variants. Eligible for holdout once the gold set grows beyond the bootstrap.

c_subfield: Bridžet Kollinz ; perevod s anglijskogo Ju. Ju. Zmejevoi
notes: Synthesized from the Russian-language extraction sample observed in the v2 5k-record measurement (real bib was 2446253). 245$c is a Russian-language record where the original-language author's name is romanized via ALA-LC ('Bridžet Kollinz' = Bridget Collins) and the translator follows after the cyrillic 'perevod' role marker. Two distinct outcomes: variant pointer for the romanized author (already in 100), new translator agent. Synthesized because the original record's full agent labels weren't preserved in the heuristic-measurement output.
